text: The clip displays an intraoperative ultrasound of the biliary tree, showing the ultrasound probe scanning over the liver area to visualize the biliary structures.
The ultrasound image displays a flow box over the aorta, indicating blood flow within the vessel.
An arrow points at the Yorda, highlighting its location in the surgical field.
The common bile duct is visible in the surgical field.
The portal vein is visible beneath both the common bile duct and the common hepatic duct.
The surgeon points out the anatomical structures forming the 'Mickey Mouse sign,' typically involving the common bile duct, hepatic artery, and portal vein.
The ultrasound image shows the common bile duct as a tubular structure at the top, which is being traced inferiorly.
The ultrasound image displays a radiopaque structure, indicating the presence of a stone within the common bile duct.
The ultrasound image is frozen, and the common bile duct is being measured.
The flow box is visible, showing no flow inside the common bile duct, which is positioned above the aorta.
The portal vein is visible slightly to the right in the surgical field.
The surgeon moves the flow box inferiorly over the aorta, revealing visible blood flow within the vessel.
The aorta is visibly pulsating beneath the surgical field.
The surgeon observes the common bile duct, noting the absence of visible flow within it, contrasting with the pulsating flow seen in the underlying aorta.
The surgeon follows the common hepatic and common bile duct inferiorly through the pancreas, observing their junction with the duodenum.
